name: Stanford Institutes of Medicine Summer Research Program (SIMR)
location: Stanford School of Medicine, Palo Alto, CA

cost.notes: Tuition-free with a stipend. Financial aid available.

essays.prompt: Describe your interest in biomedical research and what draws you to this field.
tips: SIMR alumni consistently say the key is showing genuine, specific curiosity about a research area — not just interest in going to Stanford

essays.prompt: What do you hope to learn and contribute during your 8 weeks at SIMR?
tips: Show intellectual maturity and realistic expectations of what research entails

outcomes.college_impact: One of the most prestigious biomedical research programs for high school students. Alumni report the experience as transformative for their understanding of medical science and their college applications.
outcomes.typical_results: Strong representation at Stanford, Harvard, MIT, and top medical/science programs. Many alumni publish research or present at national conferences.

what_they_look_for: Genuine, specific interest in biomedical research — not just medicine as a career concept
Strong biology and life sciences background
Maturity to work independently in a professional research environment
Teacher or mentor who can speak to the student's research potential

blurbs.short: An 8-week free research internship at Stanford School of Medicine. ~50 students are paired with Stanford faculty mentors and conduct full-time biomedical research in fields including cancer biology, neuroscience, bioengineering, and immunology. Acceptance rate ~3%.
blurbs.long: SIMR is one of the most selective biomedical research programs for high school students in the US. Each summer, approximately 50 students are selected from over 1,500 applicants (~3% acceptance rate) to spend eight weeks at the Stanford School of Medicine conducting full-time research. Students are paired with Stanford faculty mentors and work in active research labs across fields including cancer biology, neuroscience, immunology, bioengineering, and more. The program is tuition-free and provides a stipend. SIMR alumni consistently describe it as one of the most formative experiences of their high school years — giving them real insight into what biomedical research actually looks like. The program is ideal for students seriously considering medicine, biological research, or biomedical engineering.

curriculum: 8 weeks of full-time mentored research in Stanford labs; weekly seminars; professional development; final research presentations. Students experience authentic graduate-level research.
